headlines.title: ConocoPhillips (COP) Ascends While Market Falls: Some Facts to Note
headlines.summary: In the most recent trading session, ConocoPhillips (COP) closed at $120.2, indicating a +1.19% shift from the previous trading day.

headlines.title: Is Targa Resources (TRGP) Undervalued Following Its Board Update And Dividend Declaration?
headlines.summary: Targa Resources (TRGP) is back in focus after its board appointed former ConocoPhillips executive Thomas Mathiasmeier as a Class II director and Audit Committee member, following a fresh quarterly dividend declaration. See our latest analysis for Targa Resources. The recent board appointment and dividend declaration come as Targa Resources’ share price shows firm momentum, with a 30-day share price return of 7.54% and a year-to-date gain of 52.

headlines.title: Will Targa’s New LNG-Focused Director and 2026 Dividend Shape TRGP’s Capital Allocation Narrative?
headlines.summary: Targa Resources Corp. recently announced that its board appointed former ConocoPhillips executive Thomas Mathiasmeier as a Class II Director and Audit Committee member, while also declaring a second-quarter 2026 cash dividend of US$1.25 per common share, or US$5.00 per share on an annualized basis, payable on August 14, 2026.

headlines.title: ExxonMobil and Oil Stocks Rise After Rubio Says Iran ‘Not Serious’ About Talks
headlines.summary: Renewed U.S. strikes on Iran have sent oil prices higher on Wednesday, with shares in energy companies like ExxonMobil gaining as Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Iran was “not serious” about peace talks.

headlines.title: What to Expect From ConocoPhillips' Q2 2026 Earnings Report
headlines.summary: ConocoPhillips is expected to unveil its second-quarter results next month, and analysts are projecting a triple-digit rise in profit.

description: ConocoPhillips explores for, produces, transports, and markets crude oil, bitumen, natural gas, liquefied natural gas (LNG), and natural gas liquids. It operates in five segments: Alaska; Lower 48; Canada; Europe, Middle East and North Africa; and Asia Pacific. The company's portfolio includes unconventional plays in North America; conventional assets in North America, Europe, Asia, and Australia; global LNG developments; oil sands assets in Canada; and an inventory of global exploration prospects. It serves in the United States, Canada, China, Equatorial Guinea, Libya, Malaysia, Norway, Singapore, the United Kingdom, and internationally. ConocoPhillips was founded in 1917 and is headquartered in Houston, Texas.
